meta.title: Spec-Driven Development, a walk you can touch
meta.description: An interactive guide distilled from a podcast conversation: when to write a spec, when to vibe-code, and how a request really travels.

props.teaser: You made it halfway. Something practical is hiding here.

props.title: Your first spec, drafted by AI
props.template: Write a one-page spec for {{feature}}. Include: the user problem, what we will NOT build, and 3 acceptance checks a non-programmer could verify.

props.teaser: One last thing before you go.

props.children: Writing the spec first is not bureaucracy — it is the cheapest prototype you will ever build.

cta.title: Turn your podcast into interactive guides
cta.description: Get the next guides and the making-of in your inbox.

glossary.explorable explanation: A document you learn from by manipulating it and seeing the consequences (Bret Victor).
glossary.spec: A written description of intent — what to build and why — before any code.
glossary.SDD: Spec-Driven Development: write the spec first, then let it drive the code.
glossary.vibe coding: Coding by feel — describe what you want and let the AI improvise the details.

props.text: This guide blends a linear walkthrough with [[explorable explanation]] widgets. By the end you'll know when to write a [[spec]] and when to lean on [[vibe coding]].

props.children: Touching is allowed — click the diagrams and answer before you peek.

modules.title: The round trip
modules.subtitle: What happens between Enter and the page.

props.title: Hear it from the episode (2 min)

transcript.text: The slow part is almost never your CSS — it is the round trip.
Every request crosses the network, hits the server, maybe the database, and comes all the way back.

props.text: Same as code: an [[SDD]] flow writes the intention down before generating a single line.

props.question: An 8-second page load most likely comes from…
